claim_scope: the union of 20 exact 86-variable slices obtained by freeing cyclic distance 6 and exactly one other cyclic-distance orbit of the authenticated Springer K43 seed; all other 19 orbits are frozen; this is not the full 903-edge space, a changed Ramsey bound, or a global optimum claim
conclusion: No burden-zero coloring exists in any proof-checked UNSAT slice. Any SAT slice is admitted only after both complete K5 checkers report no clique and no coclique. The conclusion is limited to the specified fixed-background two-orbit union.
derivation_independence: Python uses itertools combinations and tokenized raw parsing; C separately uses five nested loops, getline parsing, independent edge-orbit indexing, and qsort deduplication. Their complete origin ledgers and canonical CNFs agree byte-for-byte for every distance.

encoding.clauses: for each five-set and target color, retain exactly when every fixed edge has that color; each literal forces a released edge away from the target color
encoding.duplicates: full originating ledger retained; DIMACS contains sorted unique clauses because multiplicity is irrelevant to burden zero
encoding.variables: 1..43 are colors of {u,u+6}; 44..86 are colors of {u,u+d}, indexed by u in Z_43

hypothesis: at least one two-orbit slice contains a burden-zero R(5,5,43) coloring

specialization_control.comparison: exact signed-clause Counter including multiplicity against the retained distance-6 ledger
